title: 沉睡谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\140-沉睡谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Gwendolyn, Jiles, and Miss Marple discuss the possibility of a married man being a third "X" in the complicated history involving Helen and Walter Fane. Miss Marple suggests that Edith Pettigrew, a former cook at St. Catherine's Manor who now lives in Dilham, might possess useful information. Despite warnings from Jiles, Gwendolyn decides to draft a new will as a pretext to get closer to Walter Fane.

Elsewhere, Lily Kimball discovers an old newspaper advertisement requesting that anyone with knowledge of Helen Spenceley-Harriet (née Kennedy) contact Reed and Hardy in Southampton Street. Lily recalls her time serving Mrs. Harriet at St. Catherine's Manor, which was rented from Miss Finsbury and her husband. Lily speculates to her husband, Jim, that Mrs. Harriet may have been murdered by her husband, noting that a suitcase, handbag, and clothes had gone missing. She remembers a Swiss nanny named Léonie witnessing something from a window during a chaotic night involving a doctor and a man sleeping in a dressing room. Lily believes Mrs. Harriet might have run off with a married lover, a theory she once shared with Edith, though Edith did not believe her.

Gwendolyn visits the law office of Walter Fane, a weary, dignified man of fifty. While discussing the signing of her simple will, Gwendolyn mentions that she and her companion have purchased a mountain villa formerly known as St. Catherine's Villa. Upon hearing this, Walter Fane removes his spectacles and looks down at the table.
